name: Sierra de la Calera and Sierra de María Andrés Loop
name_original: Sierra de la Calera y Sierra de María Andrés – circular desde La Morera
description: Explore the Sierra de la Calera y Sierra de María Andrés on a moderate 17.9-mile mountain bike loop from La Morera.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around La Morera de Montsant are characterized by the rugged terrain of the Serra de Montsant, a mountainous region known for its diverse landscapes. The area features challenging dirt and gravel roads, limestone cliffs, and varied elevation changes suitable for mountain biking. Riders can expect routes that traverse through natural features, offering panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and valleys. Best mountain bike trails around La Morera The most popular mountain bike trail is Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop, an 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de Villalba de los Barros y Ermita de San Juan – circular desde La Parra, a moderate 32.7 miles (52.7 km) path. It leads past the Embalse de Villalba de los Barros and the Ermita de San Juan. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra de la Calera and Sierra de María Andrés Loop, an 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Calera and Sierra de María Andrés mountain ranges, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around La Morera is defined by the rugged Serra de Montsant, diverse terrain including dirt and gravel roads, and views of the Siurana village and cliffs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in La Morera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Morera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around La Morera?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around La Morera, offering a variety of experiences. The region features 4 easy, 45 moderate, and 10 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in La Morera?
A: Mountain bike trails in La Morera are characterized by the rugged terrain of the Serra de Montsant. You can expect challenging dirt and gravel roads, limestone cliffs, and varied elevation changes. The routes often traverse natural features, providing panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in La Morera?
A: Yes, many routes in La Morera are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359926406/nogales-reservoir-and-12-apostles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Reservoir and 12 Apostles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.4 km (16.4 mi) ride. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030760620/sierra-de-la-calera-and-sierra-de-maria-andres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Calera and Sierra de María Andrés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 28.8 km (17.9 mi).

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking in La Morera?
A: The region offers several notable landmarks and viewpoints. You can pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1124589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Hermitage of La Parra&lt;/a&gt;, or see the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks&lt;/a&gt; are also a unique natural monument. For panoramic views, consider routes that offer a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Feria Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in La Morera?
A: While many trails in La Morera are moderate to difficult due to the rugged terrain of the Serra de Montsant, there are 4 easy routes available that might be more suitable for families. These trails typically feature less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, offering a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the area.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Montsant Natural Park?
A: The Montsant Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions that might apply to certain paths within the park.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in La Morera?
A: The mountain bike routes in La Morera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Over 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and diverse riding experiences.

Q: Are there any castles or historical sites accessible by mountain bike in the area?
A: Yes, you can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle and Church of San Cristóbal&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has a rich history, with hermitages like the Hermitage of Saint John of Codolar, built by Carthusian monks, adding to the historical appeal.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Morera?
A: La Morera, situated in Catalonia, Spain, generally offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round cycling, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in La Morera?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. In La Morera de Montsant, you can typically find parking in or near the village. It's advisable to check specific route starting points on komoot for detailed information on parking facilities to plan your ride effectively.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in La Morera?
A: La Morera de Montsant, being a village in a popular outdoor region, offers some amenities. You can find local cafes and pubs for refreshments, and there are accommodation options available, ranging from guesthouses to rural hotels, suitable for visitors looking to stay overnight.

Q: Can I reach La Morera by public transport for mountain biking?
A: While La Morera de Montsant is a rural area, it is generally accessible by public transport, though options might be limited. It's best to check local bus schedules from larger nearby towns or cities in Catalonia, as services can vary. Bringing a bike on public transport may also have specific regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the Sierra de la Calera y Sierra de María Andrés mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in La Morera. While specific parking details for this exact trail are not readily available, you can generally find parking in or near the village center of La Morera. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de la Calera y Sierra de María Andrés trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking trails in natural areas like this are dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through areas with livestock or wildlife. Always carry water for your dog and be prepared to pack out any waste. Specific regulations for this route are not detailed, so always observe local guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location, spring and autumn are typically the best seasons for mountain biking this route. The weather is usually milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potentially colder, wetter conditions of winter. This allows for a more comfortable and enjoyable ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within the Sierra María Andrés Special Area of Conservation?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Sierra María Andrés Special Area of Conservation. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within such areas in Spain. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to protect the natural environment.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' difficulty for mountain biking. While specific terrain details are not available, a moderate rating typically implies a mix of unpaved roads, gravel paths, and potentially some singletrack sections with varied gradients. Expect some climbs and descents, as indicated by the elevation gain and loss.

faqs.question: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the Sierra María Andrés Special Area of Conservation, offering beautiful natural scenery. You can also enjoy views from the Valdihuelo Viewpoint and potentially see Almond Trees in Bloom at Los Carrascales, especially if you visit in late winter or early spring.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino de La Parra, Camino de los Hitos, Camino de las Viñas, Camino de los Hornos, Camino de la Fuente, Camino del Cementerio, Camino de los Corrales, and the Camino de la Torre de Miguel Sesmero a la Morera.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: With a 'moderate' difficulty rating, this route might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners in mountain biking due to its distance (approximately 28.8 km) and elevation changes (around 470m up). Riders should have a reasonable level of fitness and some mountain biking experience. For families with older, experienced children, it could be a rewarding adventure.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail in La Morera?
faqs.answer: La Morera is a small village, and while specific establishments directly on the trail are unlikely, you can typically find local cafes or restaurants in the village itself. It's always a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially if you plan to visit during off-peak seasons or weekdays.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain bike route, essential items include plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sunscreen, and a first-aid kit. Given the potential for varied terrain, sturdy mountain biking shoes are also recommended. Always carry your phone for emergencies and navigation.

faqs.question: What is the typical weather like for mountain biking in the Sierra de la Calera y Sierra de María Andrés area?
faqs.answer: The region generally experiences a Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild winters. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for cycling. Always check the local weather forecast for La Morera before your ride, as conditions can vary, and be prepared for sun exposure, especially during warmer months.
